methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $1M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axial Composites is a seed-stage company operating in Robotics & Manufacturing. The broader sector includes 9 comparable peers (1 public, 8 private), with notable private references including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The company has raised $1M+ to date.

The valuation analysis applies a single triangulation method. Stage-progression analysis uses the $1M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied value of $2M. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient narrow-peer datapoints in the robotics & manufacturing segment; while 11 same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) needed to support a robust median calculation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0M to $3.0M, with a central estimate of $2.5M. This range reflects a modest band around the stage-progression result, accounting for typical seed-round variance. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer comparables to validate the 2.5x multiplier against robotics-specific stage progression norms, and limited visibility into Axial's specific technical or commercial milestones relative to the reference peer set.
